$comment: Azure bandwidth / peering pricing snapshot. Grounded in the public Azure Bandwidth Pricing page. Numbers are USD per GB unless stated otherwise. Refresh via 'Get-EgressRates.ps1 -Refresh'.
notes: VNet peering ingress and egress are billed at the same rate on both sides of the peering. So a peered pair moving 100 GB in one direction costs 2 x rate x 100 GB.
In-region VNet peering rate is a flat published rate. Cross-region rates vary by the source (or destination) zone (Zone 1 = US/Europe/CA, Zone 2 = APAC/JP/AU, Zone 3 = SA/India/MEA, Zone 4 = Africa).
Rates below are the standard published rates. Enterprise Agreement / MCA customers may see negotiated discounts; use these as directional inputs.
For customer-facing traffic terminated at Front Door / CDN, the internet egress rate applies (not the peering rate).
Refresh cadence: rates change infrequently (~annual). Snapshot updated in-place when a customer requests a recent baseline.

zone1.$comment: US, Europe, Canada, UK, Mexico. Cheapest egress zone.

in_region.$comment: Same-region VNet peering (spoke to hub in the same region). Charged per side, both ingress and egress.

cross_region_within_zone1.$comment: Cross-region VNet peering within Zone 1 (e.g. NE spoke <-> Sweden Central hub).

cross_region_zone1_to_zone2.$comment: Cross-region VNet peering Zone 1 -> Zone 2 (e.g. WE -> SEA).

cross_region_zone1_to_zone3.$comment: Cross-region VNet peering Zone 1 -> Zone 3 (e.g. NE -> Central India).

internet_egress_zone1.$comment: Data transfer out to the internet from Zone 1 (Europe, US, UK). Tiered.

$comment_derivation: For the scoring tool the primary signal is the peering rate delta between in-region ($0.01/GB) and cross-region ($0.02-$0.08/GB depending on zones). Score-AzureRegionFit.ps1 uses this delta to compute the incremental egress cost of putting a new workload in the candidate region while keeping the hub/database in the source region.
